Re:
Fern Por-rrrcAr- PnACTrcEs C oMMrssroN
418 J Street o Suite 6f0 r Sacramento, CA 95gl,l-1329
(916) 3l:-5660 o Fa" (916) 322-0886
Ar-rgtrst 29,2008
Holly La Rue
2866 Hickory Wood Lane #28
Thousand Oaks. CA91362
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-141
Dear Ms. La Rue:
This letter responds to your reqllest for advice regarding the reporting provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' you shoulcl not constnre this tetteias assistance
on any conduct that may have already taken place. (See Regularion 18329(bX8)(A).) In
addition, we base this letter on the facts presented. The Fair Political practices
Conltnission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
assistance. (ln re Oglesbv (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Please note that we base our advice
solely on the provisions of the Act.
QUESTION
What are your reporting and other obligations untler the Act for money that your
ex-husband pays yoLl that you use for hor-rsehold expenses?
CONCLUSION
As to that part of the payment that is for the fair market value of the meals you
cook fbr your ex-husband, you are not required to report this incorne because your ex-
hlrsband lives and works outside your city council's jurisdiction. The remainder is a gift
strbject to the -eift limits. Also, if yoLr are elected, income of $500 and gifts cumulating to
$390 or more received frorn your ex-husband during the l2-month period prior to your
participation in any -qovenlment decision having a financial effect on your-ex-husband
nlay create a conflict of interest lbr you under the Act.

Code of Regtrlations. All rc'-sulatrlry ret'erenees are to Title l. Division 6 of the Calitirrnia Cocle of
Regulations. unless rltherwise inclicated.


File No. A-08-14l
Pa-ee No. 2
FACTS
You are mnning for city council in Thousand Oaks, Califomia. You receive
money from your ex-husband based on an informal a-qreement that is outside your
divorce settlement. You cook vegetarian meals for your ex-husband and deliver them on
a weekly basis. You use the money he gives you to support your household, including
buying the food that goes into the meals you cook for him. You stated during our
telephone conversation that, while the payments are not directly remitted forihe cooking
you do, yoll suspect the payments would stop if you stopped cooking. He does not live in
your jurisdiction, nor does he do business there. You now ask whether this money is
reportable income as income or a gift under the Act and if the Act's gift limits would
apply.
ANALYSIS
You are a candidate for city council in the city of Thousand Oaks. A candidate
for city council is required to file a Statement of Economic Interest ("SEI") no later than
the final filing date for his or her declaration of candidacy. (Sections 87200 and 87201.)
The SEI must generally report, among other things, all income of $500, received during
the l2-month period before the filing, from each person doing business in the jurisdiction
and all gifts from a person, cumulating to $50 or more in value received during the lZ-
month period before the filing. (Sections 87207,82028, and 82030.) This infirmation
must also be reported on your SEI if you are elected to the city council. (Section 87200,
87202-87210.)
The Act defines "income" asl
. . . a payment received, including but not limited to any salary,
wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any sale,
gift, including any gift of food or beverage, loan, forgiveness or
payment of indebtedness received by the filer, reimbursement for
expenses, per diem, or contribution to an insurance or pension
program paid by any person other than an employer, and including
' 
any community property interest in the income of a spouse.
(Section 82030(a).)2 Your ex-husband provides payments to you on an irregular basis to
assist with your household bills. You continue to cook for your ex-husband and deliver
meals to him on a weekly basis. To the extent that the money he pays you is in
consideration for the meals that you cook for him and deliver to him, ii is income. and
normally would be reportable on your SEI, but because your ex-husband does not live or
I Excluded tiom the Act's definition of income are palments tirr spousal support (Section
li2()30(b)(7)). excepting. albeit inconectly. "alimony" fiom that deflnition. If you und y,,u, ex-husband had
a spousal support arrangement. it would be neither income nor a gift.


File No. A-08-l4l
Page No. 3
do business in your jr-rrisdiction it is not reportable. (Section g2030(a).)r If the amou,t is
over and above the fair market value of home-clelivered, vegetarian meals, the remainder
is a gift ro yolr.
The Act defines a gift as "a payment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received.,,
(Section 82028(a).) In addition to the reporting."quil"-.nrs discussed above, no
candidate for city council or an individuil elected tt that office *oy o.."pigift, from o
single source with a total value over g390a^d_ufng the period of his or t ei.ilAlOocy, or,
if elected, in a calendar year. (Secrion g9305(b)(1); Rigulatio n lg94o.2(.).) io the
extent that the payments are in excess of the value you lrovide to him, they'are
prohibited if that amolrnt exceeds $390 during the ieriod of your 
"onaiao"y io, "u.t,
calendar year if you are elected). A gift is neither accepted nor receiveA if it is returned,
donated, or 'paid down' within 30 days after receipt ofine gift. (Regulation lg9a3(a).)
Also, if you are elected to the city council, the money from your ex_husband
could create a conflict of interest in certain situations. If your husband is doing business
in the jurisdiction, and the amount of the income is over $soo, for example. thJre could
be a conflict of interest if yotr have a decision before you that financially affects yoLrr ex-
husband or his business. (Regulation 18703.3.) or, ii the gift amount is $390 or more
during the l2-month period bgfore you participate in u gou!**"nt decision financially
affecting your ex-husband or his busine.ss, a conflict oflnterest could also arise
depending on the factors involved and the- decision before you. (Regulation 1g703.4.)
should such a situation arise, please feel free to contact us igain.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
rUJt,, l,l,{.u,
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
HMR jgl

time any statement or other action is required under this title...

Index. (Section U9503(f).)
